output:
Implemented T3 in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`: stale shot-group IDs are now consumed as typed props, `hasStaleVideo` is derived into `PositionedShotGroup`, the shot-group menu payload includes stale state, and an amber `RefreshCw` badge opens the group context menu when a newer final video exists. Checkpointed batch progress to `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_2.json`.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. The full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module still has the same pre-existing pinned-shot expectation failures already noted in the prior batch (`data-shot-group-kind` assertions and `Unpin` label expectations).
deviations: The full `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module still fails on pre-existing pinned-shot assertions unrelated to the new stale badge: missing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` expectations and stale `Unpin` menu-label expectations.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified TimelineCanvas now accepts `staleShotGroupIds`, derives `hasStaleVideo` per positioned shot group via the `${shotId}:${rowId}` key, threads that state into `ShotGroupMenuState`, and renders an amber `RefreshCw` badge that opens the shot-group context menu while remaining suppressed when `hasActiveTask` is true.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The stale badge uses the same absolute overlay placement, `zIndex: 9`, and `pointerEvents: 'auto'` pattern as the final-video badge, and it is hidden when `hasActiveTask` is true so it does not compete with the active-task indicator.
